title: कुबेर हृदयम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री कुबेर हृदयम् ॥

ॐ कुबेराय विद्महे धनाधिपाय धीमहि।
तन्नो कुबेरः प्रचोदयात्॥

हृदये मे सदा तिष्ठन् कुबेरो धनदो विभुः।
मम सर्वधनं रक्षेन्नवनिधिपतिः सदा॥ १॥

वैश्रवणो महायक्षो मम कोशं प्रपूरयेत्।
अलकाधिपतिश्चैव धनं मे वर्धयेत् सदा॥ २॥

शंखनिधिं पद्मनिधिं महापद्मनिधिं तथा।
मकरं कच्छपं मुकुन्दं कुन्दं नीलं च शंखकम्॥ ३॥

नवनिधीन् मम गृहे स्थापयेत् धनदो विभुः।
सुवर्णं रजतं ताम्रं रत्नानि विविधानि च॥ ४॥

धान्यं धनं गोधनं च भूमिं गृहं च वाहनम्।
वस्त्रमाभरणं चैव सर्वं मे कुबेरो ददातु॥ ५॥

दारिद्र्यं मे विनश्यतु ऋणं मे विलयं व्रजेत्।
सम्पत्तिर्वर्धतां नित्यं कुबेरस्य प्रसादतः॥ ६॥

इदं हृदयं कुबेरस्य यः पठेत् सततं नरः।
धनधान्यसमृद्धोऽसौ कुबेरसमो भवेत्॥ ७॥

॥ इति श्री कुबेर हृदयम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Kubera Hridayam ||

Om Kuberaya Vidmahe Dhanadhipaya Dhimahi |
Tanno Kuberah Prachodayat ||

Hridaye Me Sada Tishthan Kubero Dhanado Vibhuh |
Mama Sarva Dhanam Rakshennava Nidhi Patih Sada || 1 ||

Vaishravano Mahayaksho Mama Kosham Prapurayet |
AlakAdhipatish Chaiva Dhanam Me Vardhayet Sada || 2 ||

Shankha Nidhim Padma Nidhim Mahapadma Nidhim Tatha |
Makaram Kachchhapam Mukundam Kundam Nilam Cha Shankhakam || 3 ||

Nava Nidhin Mama Grihe Sthapayet Dhanado Vibhuh |
Suvarnam Rajatam Tamram Ratnani Vividhani Cha || 4 ||

Dhanyam Dhanam Godhanam Cha Bhumim Griham Cha Vahanam |
Vastram Abharanam Chaiva Sarvam Me Kubero Dadatu || 5 ||

Daridryam Me Vinashyatu Rinam Me Vilayam Vrajet |
Sampatir Vardhatam Nityam Kuberasya Prasadatah || 6 ||

Idam Hridayam Kuberasya Yah Pathet Satatam Narah |
Dhanadhanya Samriddho Sau Kuberasamo Bhavet || 7 ||

|| Iti Shri Kubera Hridayam Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
मेरे हृदय में सदा स्थित रहें धनद कुबेर — नवनिधिपति मेरे सर्व धन की रक्षा करें।

वैश्रवण महायक्ष मेरा कोष भरें, अलकाधिपति मेरा धन सदा बढ़ाएँ।

शंखनिधि, पद्मनिधि, महापद्मनिधि, मकर, कच्छप, मुकुन्द, कुन्द, नील, शंख — नव निधियाँ मेरे घर में स्थापित करें धनद।

सुवर्ण, रजत, ताम्र, विविध रत्न, धान्य, धन, गोधन, भूमि, गृह, वाहन, वस्त्र, आभरण — सब कुबेर मुझे दें।

मेरा दारिद्र्य नष्ट हो, ऋण विलय हो, कुबेर की कृपा से सम्पत्ति नित्य बढ़े।

जो इस कुबेर हृदय का निरन्तर पाठ करता है, वह धनधान्य समृद्ध होकर कुबेर के समान हो जाता है।
benefits: Heart-essence stotra of Lord Kubera for attracting all nine treasures
Names all nine nidhis - Shankha, Padma, Mahapadma, Makara etc.
Specifically invokes wealth, gold, silver, gems, property
Invokes freedom from poverty and debts
Ideal for Dhanteras, Diwali and those seeking financial prosperity
description: Dedicated to Lord Kubera, the Kubera Hridayam (कुबेर हृदयम्) is a heart-essence prayer revered in Hindu tradition. Drawn from Shiva Purana, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Regular recitation is believed to grant freedom from poverty and debts, specifically invokes wealth, gold, silver, gems, property, and name all nine nidhis - Shankha, Padma, Mahapadma, Makara etc. For best results, devotees chant this on Friday (Shukravar) and Tuesday (Mangalvar) and during Dhanteras and Diwali. Comprising 7 verses, it can be completed in approximately 4 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Kubera Hridayam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Kubera Hridayam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Kubera Hridayam (कुबेर हृदयम्) is a 'heart-essence' stotra of Lord Kubera, the divine treasurer and Yaksha-raja of the Hindu pantheon. The hridayam genre comprises short, intensely concentrated prayers that install the deity in the devotee's hridaya (heart) and elicit the deity's most essential grace. Drawn from the Shiva Purana stotra tradition, the Kubera Hridayam opens with the Kubera Gayatri (Om Kuberaya vidmahe Dhanadhipaya dhimahi tanno Kuberah prachodayat), then prays that Kubera the wealth-giver, Vaishravana the great Yaksha, and Alaka-adhipati reside permanently in the chanter's heart, fill the kosha, multiply wealth, and personally install all nine nidhis in the devotee's home. (Source: Shiva Purana; Kubera stotra tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: Which nine treasures (Nava-nidhis) are named in the Kubera Hridayam?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 3 of the Kubera Hridayam systematically names all nine Nava-nidhis under Kubera's command: Shankha-nidhi (conch), Padma-nidhi (lotus), Mahapadma-nidhi (great lotus), Makara (crocodile), Kachchhapa (tortoise), Mukunda (emerald), Kunda (jasmine), Nila (sapphire) and Shankhaka (the smaller conch — distinct from the great Shankha-nidhi). Verse 4 then prays that the wealth-giver Kubera personally install (sthapayet) all these nine nidhis in the devotee's home. The naming of every nidhi is a Tantric installation device — by uttering each name, the corresponding category of wealth is invoked and energetically anchored at the chanter's residence. (Source: Kubera Hridayam verses 3-4.)

seoFaqs.question: What specific forms of wealth are invoked by the Kubera Hridayam?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 5 of the Kubera Hridayam offers the most exhaustive wealth-invocation in any Kubera stotra. Suvarnam (gold), Rajatam (silver), Tamram (copper), Ratnani vividhani (gems of every kind), Dhanyam (grain), Dhanam (cash wealth), Godhanam (cattle / livestock wealth), Bhumim (land), Griham (house / dwelling), Vahanam (vehicles), Vastram (clothing) and Abharanam (jewellery) — all are explicitly enumerated and prayed for. Verse 6 then prays for the destruction of daridrya (poverty), the dissolution (vilaya) of rinam (debts), and the perpetual increase of sampatti (prosperity) by Kubera-prasada. (Source: Kubera Hridayam verses 5-6.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should the Kubera Hridayam be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Kubera Hridayam is especially powerful on Friday (Shukravar — Lakshmi's day) and Tuesday (Mangalvar — for energetic action), and is most auspicious on Dhanteras (Kartik Krishna Trayodashi — the wealth-festival before Diwali), throughout the five days of Diwali (Lakshmi-Kubera puja night being the central evening), Akshaya Tritiya (Vaishakha Shukla Tritiya), Pushya nakshatra days, and Navaratri Maha-Ashtami. The chanter sits facing north (Kubera's direction), wears yellow, places a Kubera yantra or image before the chanting seat, offers naivedya of mishtanna (sweets), and recites the seven verses with deep visualization of Kubera installed in the heart. 108 repetitions are prescribed for serious sankalpa. (Source: Kubera Tantra paddhati; Dhanteras tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the benefits of reciting the Kubera Hridayam?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti of the Kubera Hridayam (verse 7) declares that whoever continuously recites this hridayam becomes dhana-dhanya-samriddha (filled with wealth and grain) and Kubera-sama (equal to Kubera himself). Traditional benefits include: divine installation of all nine nidhis in one's home; bestowal of gold, silver, copper, gems, grain, livestock, land, dwellings, vehicles, clothing and ornaments; complete destruction of poverty (daridrya-nasha) and dissolution of debts (rina-vilaya); the permanent residence of Kubera in the devotee's heart; perpetual increase of prosperity by Kubera-prasada; and especially powerful results when chanted on Dhanteras and Diwali by householders, business-owners and seekers of financial liberation. (Source: Kubera Hridayam phalashruti.)

seoFaqs.question: How does the Kubera Hridayam differ from the Kubera Ashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Kubera Hridayam and Kubera Ashtakam are complementary wealth-prayers with distinct ritual functions. The Hridayam is a 'heart-essence' stotra (7 verses, ~4 minutes) whose entire purpose is hridaya-nyasa — installing Kubera permanently in the devotee's heart and naming the nine nidhis for direct manifestation. The Ashtakam (8 verses, ~7 minutes) is a more traditional stuti that praises Kubera with multiple epithets, includes a fuller dhyana shloka, names the nine nidhis with more poetic elaboration, and includes a longer phalashruti. Devotees often recite both together: the Hridayam for daily nityapaath and the Ashtakam for major wealth-festivals like Dhanteras and Akshaya Tritiya. (Source: classical Kubera stotra-paddhati.)
